questions.q: Lal Bahadur Shastri served as which numbered Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Lal Bahadur Shastri was the second Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: In which year did Lal Bahadur Shastri become Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Shastri became Prime Minister in 1964, succeeding Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: Which famous slogan is associated with Lal Bahadur Shastri?
questions.explanation: Shastri coined the slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan', honouring soldiers and farmers.

questions.q: Lal Bahadur Shastri led the country during a war in which year?
questions.explanation: Shastri led India during the war of 1965.

questions.q: In which city did Lal Bahadur Shastri pass away in 1966?
questions.explanation: Shastri died in Tashkent in 1966.

questions.q: Whom did Lal Bahadur Shastri succeed as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Shastri succeeded Jawaharlal Nehru as Prime Minister in 1964.

questions.q: The first part of Shastri's slogan, 'Jai Jawan', honours which group?
questions.explanation: 'Jai Jawan' salutes the jawan, the soldier.

questions.q: The second part of Shastri's slogan, 'Jai Kisan', honours which group?
questions.explanation: 'Jai Kisan' salutes the kisan, the farmer.

questions.q: In which year did Lal Bahadur Shastri's tenure as Prime Minister end?
questions.explanation: Shastri's tenure ended with his death in 1966.

questions.q: Lal Bahadur Shastri is remembered for his reputation for which quality?
questions.explanation: Shastri is widely remembered for his personal simplicity and honesty.

questions.q: Approximately how long did Lal Bahadur Shastri serve as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Shastri served as Prime Minister for roughly eighteen months, from 1964 to 1966.

questions.q: Which agreement did Shastri sign shortly before his death in 1966?
questions.explanation: Shastri signed the Tashkent Declaration in January 1966, shortly before he died there.

questions.q: Which senior cabinet portfolio did Shastri hold under Nehru before becoming Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Shastri served as Home Minister in Nehru's cabinet before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: Shastri's slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' was raised in the context of which twin national concerns?
questions.explanation: The slogan linked the soldier and the farmer, reflecting concerns of defence and food production.

questions.q: Who served as acting Prime Minister briefly after Shastri's death before a regular successor took office?
questions.explanation: Gulzarilal Nanda served as acting Prime Minister after Shastri's death, as he had after Nehru's.

questions.q: Who became the regular Prime Minister of India after Lal Bahadur Shastri?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi became the regular Prime Minister following Shastri's death in 1966.

questions.q: Shastri's tenure laid groundwork for which major agricultural transformation that followed in India?
questions.explanation: Shastri's emphasis on food self-sufficiency helped set the stage for the Green Revolution.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Lal Bahadur Shastri born?
questions.explanation: Shastri was born in Mughalsarai, in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Lal Bahadur Shastri's birthday falls on the same date as that of which other major national figure?
questions.explanation: Shastri was born on 2 October, the same date as Mahatma Gandhi's birthday.

questions.q: The honorific 'Shastri' attached to his name originally denoted what?
questions.explanation: 'Shastri' was a scholarly title he received, rather than a family surname.

questions.q: Shastri's leadership during the 1965 conflict is generally noted for which quality?
questions.explanation: Shastri is remembered for steady leadership during the 1965 war.

questions.q: Shastri was a participant in the freedom movement and was associated with which national party before independence?
questions.explanation: Shastri was a member of the Indian National Congress during the freedom struggle.

questions.q: In which month and year did Lal Bahadur Shastri pass away in Tashkent?
questions.explanation: Shastri died in Tashkent in January 1966, soon after signing the Tashkent Declaration.

questions.q: The Tashkent Declaration that Shastri signed was mediated with the help of which country?
questions.explanation: The Tashkent meeting was hosted and mediated by the Soviet Union.

questions.q: Lal Bahadur Shastri was posthumously awarded which honour, becoming the first person to receive it after death?
questions.explanation: Shastri was the first person to be awarded the Bharat Ratna posthumously.

questions.q: Before holding cabinet office at the Centre, Shastri held ministerial positions in which state's government?
questions.explanation: Shastri held ministerial office in the government of Uttar Pradesh before moving to the Centre.

questions.q: As Railways Minister earlier in his career, Shastri set a notable precedent by doing what after a major accident?
questions.explanation: Shastri resigned as Railways Minister accepting moral responsibility after a serious railway accident.

questions.q: During a food shortage, Shastri appealed to citizens to do which of the following as a voluntary gesture?
options: Skip a meal to conserve food grain
questions.explanation: Shastri appealed to citizens to voluntarily forgo a meal to help conserve food grain.

questions.q: Shastri's slogan is often understood to have boosted morale on two fronts simultaneously. Which two?
options: The battlefield and the farm field
questions.explanation: The slogan was meant to raise morale among soldiers at the front and farmers in the fields.

questions.q: Shastri became Prime Minister through which process after Nehru's death?
options: Direct popular vote for Prime Minister
Election as leader by the ruling party in Parliament
questions.explanation: Shastri was chosen as Prime Minister after being elected leader by his party in Parliament.
